response.justification: Bogdan brings genuine staff-level credentials across multiple companies, strong TypeScript depth, and current hands-on work in AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling that maps directly to this AI-native environment. However, Python — a stated must-have — is entirely absent from his resume and skills list, which is a material gap. His high-throughput distributed systems experience (Sociomantic: millions of HTTP req/s, async I/O, low-latency fiber architecture) is real but concentrated in 2018–2020; the more recent Optilyz tenure was pricing automation and front-end work, not infrastructure scale. A phone screen to validate the Python gap and probe operational/incident-response experience is warranted before a hard pass.
strengths: Current role involves AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators — genuine AI-native engineering, not just adjacent exposure
Proven high-throughput distributed systems work at Sociomantic (millions of HTTP requests/sec, async I/O, fibers, high-performance databases)
Repeated Staff-level titles with evidence of technical strategy, cross-team coordination, and community knowledge-sharing (workshops, conference talks)
concerns: Python is completely absent from the resume and skills list despite being a must-have for the role
Distributed systems depth is concentrated in 2018–2020; the four-year Optilyz tenure that follows was pricing logic and React work, not infrastructure scale
No explicit mention of on-call rotations, incident response, or operational ownership — all listed as must-have non-technical requirements

key_factors.factor: Distributed systems experience is 5+ years stale
